intro.title: Across the Continent
intro.body: Vivian Fuchs led the Commonwealth Trans-Antarctic Expedition across the continent via the South Pole — the first crossing. Edmund Hillary's New Zealand party laid the depots from McMurdo Sound. Fuchs reached Scott Base 99 days out from Shackleton Base on 2 March 1958.

stops.name: Shackleton Base (Vahsel Bay)
stops.note: British starting point on the Weddell Sea side, established 1956. Fuchs's crossing party departs here 24 November 1957.
stops.imagePrompt: Photographic plate, post-war register. Ice-shelf coast with Sno-Cats and dogsleds, prefabricated huts, RAF Otter aircraft on skis.

stops.name: South Ice (depot)
stops.note: Forward depot on the Filchner ice sheet, 555 km in. Fuchs surveys glaciological data here.
stops.imagePrompt: Photographic plate. Cluster of canvas pyramid tents on a featureless white plain, three Sno-Cats parked in line.

stops.name: South Pole
stops.note: Reached 19 January 1958. Hillary's New Zealand party arrives from the other side on 4 January, third in history after Amundsen and Scott.
stops.imagePrompt: Photographic plate. Sno-Cat at striped pole marker, Stars and Stripes flag of Amundsen-Scott station behind, drift on the ice.

stops.name: Depot 700 (Skelton Glacier)
stops.note: Hillary's depot at the top of the Skelton Glacier. The two parties meet on Fuchs's descent in February 1958.
stops.imagePrompt: Photographic plate. Tractor train on a glacier between mountain walls, fuel drums stacked in a low cairn.

stops.name: Scott Base (McMurdo)
stops.note: Fuchs arrives 2 March 1958, 99 days out from Shackleton. The first surface crossing of Antarctica is complete.
stops.imagePrompt: Photographic plate. Wooden NZ base huts on volcanic shore of Ross Island, Mount Erebus smoking behind, sea ice in the bay.
